# ADR 0002: XcodeGen with a gitignored .xcodeproj

**Status:** Accepted (2026-07)

## Context

The repo is developed primarily from the CLI (agent-driven). `.xcodeproj` files are
merge-conflict magnets and effectively unreviewable; adding files by editing `project.pbxproj`
from the command line is brittle.

## Decision

**The project is defined in `project.yml`; `Mercury.xcodeproj` is generated by XcodeGen and
gitignored** (as is the generated `Mercury/Support/Info.plist`). Membership is directory-based:
anything under `Mercury/`, `MercuryTests/`, `MercuryUITests/` is in the respective target, so
adding a file needs no project edit at all — just `xcodegen generate`.

## Consequences

- `xcodegen generate` must run after cloning and after every `project.yml` change (CI does this;
  see `.github/workflows/ci.yml`).
- Anything normally configured through Xcode's project UI (build settings, Info.plist keys,
  schemes) is expressed in `project.yml` instead — which is the reviewable record of it.
- Requires `brew install xcodegen` locally and ~1 min of brew time per CI run.
